Propose an AI edit to the document. Default behaviour auto-accepts the
proposal; --dry-run auto-rejects; --interactive shows the diff and asks.

Examples:
  pura chat xy12ab "tighten the intro"
  pura chat xy12ab "summarize in 3 bullets" --dry-run
  pura chat xy12ab "add an email column" --interactive
  pura chat xy12ab "retry" --resolve=reject

Usage:
  pura chat <slug> "<instruction>" [flags]

Flags:
      --dry-run            Auto-reject the proposal so nothing lands; use to preview
  -h, --help               help for chat
      --interactive        On TTY, show the diff and prompt before applying
      --model string       Model id; when omitted the server picks the default
      --no-stream          Suppress the token-by-token stderr stream
      --resolve string     When a pending proposal blocks this turn, resolve it first: accept|reject
      --selection string   Scope the edit to this substring of the doc
      --yes                Skip the interactive prompt (forces auto-accept)

Global Flags:
      --api-url string   Override API endpoint
      --handle string    User handle (accepts alice or @alice; defaults to _)
      --jq string        Filter JSON output with jq expression
      --json             Force JSON output
      --profile string   Use named profile
      --quiet            Raw data only (no envelope)
      --token string     Override stored token
  -v, --verbose          Verbose output
